rationale: The US-Iran conflict is actively escalating with new strikes and threats, posing indirect but real risk to Poland via energy prices and regional destabilization; all other events are continuations or geographically distant.

top_events.title: US escalates strikes on Iran, warns of more attacks
top_events.summary: Trump announced further strikes on Iran 'today' and warned of continued action after a US Apache helicopter was downed; Iran vows to defend itself. This active escalation raises energy price risk and regional instability concerns that indirectly affect Poland's economy and security environment.

top_events.title: US inflation hits three-year high amid energy price surge
top_events.summary: US inflation has reached a new three-year high, driven partly by rising energy prices linked to Middle East tensions. This signals potential spillover to European energy markets and consumer prices, with indirect impact on Polish households.

top_events.title: Ukraine strikes military plant deep inside Russia
top_events.summary: Ukraine reports successful missile strikes on a military plant deep inside Russian territory, continuing its long-range strike campaign. This is a continuation of the established pattern with no qualitative change in the conflict's impact on Poland.
